prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `cb227835`.
codex_response:
The rule is: keep the two blue cells fixed and add green cells as the outline of a thin slanted rectangle whose blue cells are opposite corners. When the row and column gaps differ, the longer axis contributes straight horizontal or vertical end stretches, but it is still the same single geometric construction.

I used the `arc2_opus46_summary.json` hint because it matched the official examples, and I discarded the `arc2_sonnet45_summary.jsonl` hint as misleading; that correction is recorded in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_cb227835/notes.md). The generator stays on-family by sampling only empty black grids with exactly two blue singleton anchors, then varying slope sign, dominant axis, span, and edge placement to produce the same tilted-outline family seen in the official task. No new shared primitive was needed.

Validation passed: official verifier `4/4`, generated examples `1000/1000`, build succeeded, and I visually checked [preview_01.png](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/artifacts/cb227835/previews/preview_01.png).
